evidence_quotes.quote: make sure people can still access games theyve bought, even after official servers are shut down.
offer options like offline play, open-sourcing, or letting the community host servers
payment providers can refuse service because of "moral clauses" or other personal policies, even when nothing illegal has happened.
If service is denied, there should be clear reasons and a fair way to appeal or fix the issue.

paragraphs.text: I would like to kindly ask that the Digital Fairness Act include a few important protections for everyone in the EUs digital economy. First, I ask that the Act require game publishers and platforms to make sure people can still access games theyve bought, even after official servers are shut down. Too often, players lose access to games they paid for just because a company decides to end online support. This does not feel fair to consumers, and it also means that part of our digital culture and history can disappear forever. Video games are not just products, but also a valuable form of culture and art. I kindly ask that the Act include a rule for publishers to offer options like offline play, open-sourcing, or letting the community host servers, so that people keep access to what they bought and we protect our digital cultural heritage. Second, I am asking nicely for the Act to protect platforms and creators from being excluded by payment processors for subjective reasons. At the moment, payment providers can refuse service because of "moral clauses" or other personal policies, even when nothing illegal has happened. This kind of decision should not be left up to private companies and can result in unfair discrimination or censorship. I would appreciate it if the Act would make sure payment processors can only deny service when a law is actually being broken, not just because they disagree with the content or ideas. If service is denied, there should be clear reasons and a fair way to appeal or fix the issue. Both of these suggestions are about fairness, transparency, and protecting peoples rights and our culture in the digital world. I am asking for these protections to be added to the Digital Fairness Act because they will help everyone: consumers, creators, and the whole community. Thank you very much for considering my request and for your work on this important law.
